findings.description: Nested <main> landmark elements - ensure the document has at most one main landmark at top level
findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 40. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: Ensure that lists are structured correctly
findings.evidence:
Fix all of the following:
  List element has direct children that are not allowed: figure [<ol type="I">]; Fix all of the following:
  List element has direct children that are not allowed: figure [<ol type="A">]

findings.description: Page 2 (HTML) inserts repeated header/footer content (State of North Carolina | Division of Marine Fisheries address block) between sections, breaking the reading flow mid-document.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, page 2 content flows continuously from Onslow County section D through Brunswick County. In the HTML, the footer block and then a repeated header (ROY COOPER, Governor, ELIZABETH S. BISER, Secretary, KATHY B. RAWLS, Director, PT-2-2024, PROCLAMATION title, and the RE: line) appear between the end of page 1 content and the continuation on page 2.

findings.description: The HTML inserts a large blank area on what corresponds to page 2, with repeated masthead/header information that was not part of the document's reading content in the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2 begins directly with 'Proclamation PT-2-2024 Pg. 2' and continues with section content. HTML equivalent shows a full-page blank space with only a footer, then re-renders the header block (ROY COOPER, ELIZABETH S. BISER, KATHY B. RAWLS, PT-2-2024, PROCLAMATION, RE: line) before continuing with the content.

findings.description: Repeated footer and header content appears between the Brunswick County section and the General Information section in the HTML, interrupting reading flow.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, page 3 follows continuously from Brunswick County to General Information section X. In the HTML, the State of North Carolina footer block appears again between Brunswick County section E and General Information section X.

findings.description: The HTML has nested <main> elements - there is a <main> in the outer body template and another <main> generated inside the content, creating invalid and confusing structure.
findings.evidence: The outer template has <body><main>...</main></body> but the converted content also wraps in <main>...</main>, resulting in nested main elements.

findings.description: Page footer boilerplate (State of North Carolina | Division of Marine Fisheries | 3441 Arendell Street...) appears as embedded images between content sections rather than being removed or consolidated.
findings.evidence: Two embedded images appear between content sections (after Section VI.C and after Section IX.E) that appear to be page footers/headers from the PDF.

findings.description: Section headings for counties (V through X) are rendered as underlined list items rather than proper headings. While the text is correct, the heading hierarchy is flat - there is only one h1 ('PROCLAMATION') and no h2/h3 for major sections like county names and 'GENERAL INFORMATION'.
findings.evidence: PDF sections V (CARTERET COUNTY/ONSLOW COUNTY), VI (ONSLOW COUNTY), VII (PENDER COUNTY), VIII (NEW HANOVER COUNTY), IX (BRUNSWICK COUNTY), X (GENERAL INFORMATION) are all bold/underlined section headers in the PDF but rendered as <li><u>...</u> in the HTML with no heading tags.

findings.description: The typo 'UNLAWFULLTO' exists in both the PDF and HTML, so this is not a conversion error - it is faithfully reproduced.
findings.evidence: Both PDF and HTML contain 'IT SHALL BE UNLAWFULLTO USE POTS' - this is in the source document.
